seo.title: Government Voice Agents AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for voice agents AI agents in government. Covers FedRAMP, FISMA, NIST 800-53.

content.intro: Deploying voice AI agents in government contexts carries extraordinary stakes: a misconfigured speech pipeline or unvetted LLM response can trigger national security incidents, civil liberties violations, and congressional scrutiny. Federal deployments must satisfy overlapping mandates from FedRAMP, FISMA, NIST 800-53, and the Executive Order on AI while maintaining sub-second latency for real-time citizen interactions. This checklist provides concrete, actionable controls to ensure your government voice agent is secure, explainable, and compliant before a single citizen call is processed.

sections.heading: FedRAMP & FISMA Authorization Controls
sections.description: Ensure every component in the speech-to-text → LLM → text-to-speech pipeline holds the appropriate federal authorization before processing any government data.

items.label: Verify FedRAMP Authorization for All Pipeline Components
items.description: Confirm that your STT provider, LLM inference endpoint, and TTS service each hold a current FedRAMP Moderate or High Authorization to Operate (ATO). Document the authorization package numbers and expiration dates in your system security plan.

items.label: Obtain Agency-Level ATO Before Production Launch
items.description: Complete the full FISMA ATO process with your agency's Authorizing Official (AO), including a System Security Plan (SSP), Security Assessment Report (SAR), and Plan of Action & Milestones (POA&M) specific to the voice agent architecture.

items.label: Classify Data Impact Level and Enforce Boundary Controls
items.description: Formally classify all voice data processed by the agent (Low, Moderate, or High impact per FIPS 199). Ensure the deployment boundary matches the classification — no classified or CUI audio may be routed to commercial LLM APIs outside a FedRAMP High or IL4/IL5 boundary.

items.label: Implement Continuous Monitoring per FISMA Requirements
items.description: Deploy automated monitoring tools that report security metrics to the agency's ISCM program monthly. Include voice pipeline-specific metrics: STT error rates, LLM response anomalies, and TTS output integrity checks.

items.label: Document All Third-Party AI Service Interconnections
items.description: Create an Interconnection Security Agreement (ISA) and Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) for every external AI service in the pipeline. Include data flow diagrams showing exactly where citizen voice data travels and at what encryption state.

items.label: Conduct Annual FISMA Security Reviews for Voice Pipeline Changes
items.description: Treat any model update, STT provider change, or latency optimization that alters data flow as a significant change requiring re-assessment under FISMA. Maintain a change log tied to the SSP.

items.label: Enforce Federal Data Residency — All Voice Data Stored in US Regions Only
items.description: Configure STT, LLM, and TTS services to store and process all audio and transcript data exclusively in US-based data centers. Validate this with contractual guarantees and technical controls, not just vendor assurances.

sections.heading: NIST 800-53 Technical Security Controls for Voice Pipelines
sections.description: Apply the specific NIST 800-53 control families most critical to real-time voice AI: access control, audit logging, system integrity, and incident response — tailored to the unique attack surface of STT/LLM/TTS pipelines.

items.label: Encrypt All Voice Data In-Transit with TLS 1.3 Minimum
items.description: Enforce TLS 1.3 on every hop: caller → STT endpoint, STT → LLM, LLM → TTS, TTS → caller. Disable TLS 1.1 and 1.2. Map this control to NIST 800-53 SC-8 (Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity) in your SSP.

items.label: Implement Immutable Audit Logs for Every Voice Interaction
items.description: Log every STT transcript, LLM prompt/response pair, and TTS output with timestamps, session IDs, and citizen identifiers (anonymized per PII policy). Store logs in a write-once, SIEM-integrated system. Maps to AU-2, AU-9, AU-12.

items.label: Apply Least-Privilege Access Controls to LLM Inference Endpoints
items.description: Restrict LLM API keys and inference endpoint access to only the voice agent service account. Use IAM roles with time-limited tokens, not static credentials. Rotate credentials every 90 days. Maps to AC-2, AC-6.

items.label: Deploy Prompt Injection Detection at the STT-to-LLM Boundary
items.description: Implement an input validation layer between the STT transcript and LLM prompt construction that detects and blocks adversarial injection attempts (e.g., a caller saying 'ignore previous instructions'). Log all blocked attempts for incident review. Maps to SI-10.

items.label: Establish Voice Agent Incident Response Runbook
items.description: Create a specific IR runbook for voice agent failures: LLM hallucination producing harmful citizen guidance, STT misrecognition of sensitive data, or TTS outputting PII. Define escalation paths to CISO within 1 hour of detection. Maps to IR-4, IR-8.

items.label: Conduct Penetration Testing on Voice Pipeline Before Go-Live
items.description: Commission a red team exercise specifically targeting the voice pipeline: adversarial audio inputs to STT, prompt injection via spoken commands, and TTS output manipulation. Remediate all critical and high findings before ATO. Maps to CA-8.

items.label: Implement Session Timeout and Re-Authentication for Extended Calls
items.description: Automatically terminate voice sessions after 15 minutes of inactivity and require re-authentication for sessions exceeding 30 minutes when handling sensitive citizen data. Maps to AC-12, IA-11.

items.label: Validate LLM Model Integrity with Cryptographic Checksums
items.description: Before each deployment, verify the cryptographic hash of the LLM model weights or API version against a known-good baseline. Alert and halt deployment if checksums do not match. Maps to SI-7 (Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity).

sections.heading: Executive Order on AI & Algorithmic Accountability
sections.description: Comply with the federal AI Executive Order requirements for algorithmic impact assessments, explainability, and citizen appeal rights — operationalized for the specific decision-making patterns of a government voice agent.

items.label: Complete Algorithmic Impact Assessment Before Deployment
items.description: Conduct a formal AIA documenting: what decisions the voice agent influences, which citizen populations are affected, potential disparate impacts by race/language/disability, and mitigation measures. Submit to agency AI governance board for approval before go-live.

items.label: Implement Explainability Logging for All Citizen-Affecting Decisions
items.description: For every LLM response that affects a citizen outcome (benefit eligibility, service routing, information provided), log the reasoning chain, confidence score, and data sources used. This log must be retrievable within 48 hours for any citizen appeal or FOIA request.

items.label: Build a Human Escalation Path Accessible Within Every Voice Interaction
items.description: Ensure citizens can say 'speak to a human' or 'I want to appeal this' at any point in the conversation and be transferred within 30 seconds. The voice agent must never block or discourage escalation. Document this path in the AIA.

items.label: Prohibit Voice Agent from Making Final Determinations on Benefits or Enforcement
items.description: Configure the LLM system prompt and output guardrails to explicitly prevent the voice agent from issuing final determinations on benefit eligibility, fines, or enforcement actions. All such outcomes must be flagged for human review before being communicated as final.

items.label: Conduct Bias Testing Across Demographic Groups and Accents
items.description: Test the STT component for word error rate disparities across accents, dialects, and languages spoken by your citizen population. Test the LLM for differential response quality across demographic groups. Document results in the AIA and remediate disparities exceeding 5% WER difference.

items.label: Publish a Plain-Language AI Use Notice for Citizens
items.description: At the start of every voice interaction, disclose that the citizen is speaking with an AI system, what data is being collected, and how to request human assistance. This disclosure must be in the citizen's preferred language if detectable.

items.label: Register the Voice Agent in the Agency AI Inventory
items.description: Add the voice agent to your agency's AI use case inventory as required by the EO on AI and OMB guidance. Include system description, risk tier, AIA status, and responsible AI officer contact. Update the inventory within 30 days of any significant system change.

sections.heading: Section 508 Accessibility & Voice Quality Assurance
sections.description: Ensure the voice agent meets Section 508 accessibility mandates and passes rigorous quality verification for response appropriateness, latency budgets, conversation coherence, and safety filtering before serving any citizen.

items.label: Achieve Sub-400ms End-to-End Latency Under Load Testing
items.description: Benchmark the full STT → LLM → TTS pipeline under simulated peak load (e.g., 500 concurrent calls). Latency at the 95th percentile must not exceed 400ms for STT response and 800ms for full LLM + TTS response. Fail the deployment if thresholds are exceeded.

items.label: Implement Real-Time Safety Filtering on LLM Outputs Before TTS
items.description: Deploy a dedicated safety classifier between the LLM output and TTS input that blocks responses containing: PII of other citizens, legally incorrect benefit information, discriminatory content, or instructions that could cause citizen harm. Log all filtered outputs for review.

items.label: Provide TTY/TDD Compatibility and Text Transcript Alternative
items.description: Ensure the voice agent system supports TTY/TDD relay services for deaf and hard-of-hearing citizens per Section 508 §1194.23. Offer a real-time text transcript of the conversation accessible via web or SMS as an alternative channel.

items.label: Support Multilingual Interactions for Populations with Limited English Proficiency
items.description: Per Executive Order 13166, configure the voice agent to detect and respond in the top languages spoken by your agency's citizen population. At minimum, support Spanish. Validate STT and TTS quality for each supported language with native speaker testing.

items.label: Implement Conversation Coherence Monitoring with Automatic Fallback
items.description: Deploy a coherence scoring module that evaluates whether the LLM's response is contextually appropriate to the citizen's query. If coherence score drops below threshold (e.g., 0.7) for two consecutive turns, automatically offer human escalation and log the session for review.

items.label: Conduct Section 508 Conformance Testing with Assistive Technology Users
items.description: Before launch, conduct usability testing with at least 5 participants who use assistive technologies (screen readers, voice control, TTY). Document findings and remediate any barriers to access. Obtain a Section 508 conformance certification from your agency's 508 coordinator.

items.label: Validate Response Appropriateness with Domain Expert Review Panel
items.description: Assemble a panel of 3+ subject matter experts (e.g., benefits specialists, legal counsel) to evaluate 500+ sampled voice agent responses for factual accuracy, legal correctness, and tone appropriateness. Achieve >95% appropriateness rating before go-live.

items.label: Implement Graceful Degradation When LLM Confidence is Low
items.description: Configure the voice agent to recognize low-confidence LLM outputs (e.g., ambiguous citizen queries, out-of-scope topics) and respond with a scripted, legally-reviewed fallback message rather than a potentially incorrect AI-generated response. Define confidence thresholds in the system design document.

notes.note: Every AI service in your voice pipeline — including third-party STT and TTS APIs — must independently hold FedRAMP authorization at the impact level matching your data classification. A FedRAMP Moderate ATO does not cover processing of CUI or data classified at the High impact level. Verify authorization status at marketplace.fedramp.gov and re-verify after any vendor infrastructure change.

NIST 800-53 Rev 5 introduced new controls directly applicable to AI systems, including SA-22 (Unsupported System Components) and SR-3 (Supply Chain Controls). For voice AI, this means you must assess the supply chain risk of your LLM provider's training data and model provenance, not just their infrastructure security. Document this assessment in your SSP under the SA and SR control families.

notes.regulation: Executive Order 14110 on Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy AI
notes.note: EO 14110 requires federal agencies to designate a Chief AI Officer, conduct AI impact assessments, and report high-impact AI use cases to OMB. A government voice agent that influences citizen access to services qualifies as high-impact. Ensure your AIA is completed before deployment and that the system is reported in your agency's annual AI use case inventory submission to OMB.

Section 508 applies to all electronic and information technology procured or developed by federal agencies, explicitly including voice-based interfaces. The voice agent must not create a situation where citizens with disabilities receive a lesser quality of service than those without. If the voice channel cannot be made fully accessible, an equally effective alternative means of access (e.g., text chat, human agent) must be provided and prominently offered at the start of every interaction.

cta.heading: Automate Your Government Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your government voice agent against FedRAMP, FISMA, NIST 800-53, and EO on AI requirements — catching compliance gaps before they become congressional investigations.
